# @platform/tooling-ci

CI/CD pipeline definitions: workflow files, build scripts, security scanning, and deployment orchestration.

## Package identity

```text
Name: @platform/tooling-ci
Version: 0.1.0
Component: tooling-ci
Type: library
System: enterprise-react-platform
Domain: delivery
Bounded context: ci-pipeline
Owner: team-platform
```

## Lifecycle

```text
Stage: active
Role: tooling
Class: active.tooling
Catalog lifecycle: production
Visibility: internal
Support level: standard
Review cadence: quarterly
```

## Ownership

```text
Owner: team-platform
Change control: owner-review
Promotion eligible: false
Semver policy: internal-traceable
```

## Responsibilities

- Define CI workflow files (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, or equivalent) for build, test, and deploy
- Run architecture governance checks as a required CI gate
- Invoke container image builds via tooling-docker and infrastructure applies via tooling-terraform
- Run SAST, dependency scanning, and container image scanning as CI steps

## Non-responsibilities

- Does not define what gets built (owned by individual packages)
- Does not own deployment targets or runtime infrastructure (owned by infra-aws)
- Does not own secret management for CI runners

## Public exports and usage

```text
Public exports only: true
Deep imports allowed: false
```

- CI workflow files are picked up automatically by the CI platform on push
- Local scripts can be run to validate workflow syntax before push

## Operational notes

- Architecture governance (`orchestrator all`) must pass as a required check before merge
- All secrets used in CI must be stored in the CI platform secret store, never in workflow files
